title: Havana 2-Day City Break
metaTitle: Havana 2-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Havana, Cuba: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Museo de la Revolución and Old Havana (Habana Vieja) walking tour. Prices from $445 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Havana, Cuba — Tropical climate with warm winters (highs around 26°C) and hot, humid summers (highs around 32-33°C); hurricane season runs June to November, peaking August-October. November to April is the dry season with pleasant temperatures and lower humidity, avoiding the June-November hurricane risk window. This itinerary blends Museo de la Revolución, Old Havana (Habana Vieja) walking tour, Malecón seafront promenade with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Museo de la Revolución, Old Havana (Habana Vieja) walking tour, Malecón seafront promenade
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Havana
itinerary.description: Land at José Martí International Airport (HAV) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Museo de la Revolución — Cuban Revolution history museum in the former Presidential Palace. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Havana Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Old Havana (Habana Vieja) walking tour — UNESCO World Heritage colonial old town guided tour. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to HAV for your onward flight.

travelTips: Bring all the cash you'll need in euros or another convertible currency to exchange — US-issued cards generally don't work due to the embargo.
US travelers must select and keep documentation for an OFAC-authorized travel category — pure tourism is not permitted.
Book a casa particular (private homestay) for a more authentic, often cheaper stay than state-run hotels.
Buy an internet Wi-Fi card (ETECSA) at public hotspots — most public squares and hotel lobbies have them.
Eating at paladares (private restaurants) generally means better food and service than state-run restaurants.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Bring sufficient cash — international debit/credit cards from most countries do not work in Cuba due to sanctions
Basic toiletries and medicine — imported goods are scarce and appreciated as gifts
A portable charger — power outages are not uncommon
Lightweight clothing plus a light rain jacket for humid, showery weather
thingsToDo: Fortaleza San Carlos de la Cabaña (evening cannon ceremony)
Hemingway trail tour (Finca Vigía and Cojímar)
Callejón de Hamel Afro-Cuban art tour
thingsToAvoid: Assuming any US-issued card will work — bring enough cash in euros/pounds/CAD, as ATMs are unreliable for foreign cards.
Buying cigars from unlicensed street sellers — counterfeit cigars are common; buy from official factory shops.
Openly criticizing the government in public conversations.
Relying on constant internet access — connectivity is limited, metered and often slow.
Skipping mandatory travel/health insurance — Cuba requires proof of insurance for entry.
topRestaurants: La Bodeguita del Medio — Traditional Cuban/Mojito birthplace ($$)
San Cristóbal Paladar — Cuban Fine Dining ($$$)
Paladar Los Mercaderes — Cuban Fine Dining ($$$)
shoppingAreas: Plaza de la Catedral craft stalls
Plaza de Armas secondhand book market
nightlife: La Zorra y el Cuervo jazz club
instagramSpots: Vintage convertible cars on the Malecón
hiddenGems: Callejón de Hamel Sunday rumba sessions
Regla ferry crossing across the harbor
familyActivities: Parque Lenin (outskirts) for a nature break
adventureActivities: Day trip snorkeling/diving at Playas del Este
Exploring Fortaleza San Carlos de la Cabaña ramparts
Salsa dancing lessons in Old Havana
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at the Hotel Nacional de Cuba
Private vintage convertible tour with a driver-guide
Tropicana Cabaret VIP table with premium rum
Tasting menu at San Cristóbal or La Guarida
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset convertible car ride along the Malecón
Private rooftop dinner overlooking Old Havana
Cigar and rum tasting for two
budgetExperiences: Free sunset stroll along the Malecón
Mojito at La Bodeguita del Medio (its birthplace) for a modest price
Free wander through Plaza Vieja and Plaza de la Catedral
Callejón de Hamel Sunday rumba, donation-based
localEtiquette: Cubans are warm and expressive — greetings with a handshake or cheek kiss (between women, or man-woman) are common.
Tipping in cash (USD, EUR or CUP) is important and appreciated — many service workers rely heavily on tips given low state wages.
Bring small gifts (toiletries, medicine, school supplies) if visiting a casa particular — genuinely useful given import restrictions.
Avoid loud political commentary about the government in public settings out of respect and caution.
Internet access is limited and metered via Wi-Fi cards/hotspots in public squares — don't expect always-on connectivity.
localTransportGuide: Classic taxis, official state taxis and the hop-on-hop-off Habana Bus Tour cover most visitor needs, since public buses are crowded and run unpredictably. Walking is the best way to explore Old Havana itself, and pre-arranging casa particular or hotel transfers avoids haggling confusion on arrival.
bestTimeToVisit: November to April is the dry season with pleasant temperatures and lower humidity, avoiding the June-November hurricane risk window.
weather: Tropical climate with warm winters (highs around 26°C) and hot, humid summers (highs around 32-33°C); hurricane season runs June to November, peaking August-October.
visaInformation: Most visitors need a Cuban Tourist Card (visa) arranged in advance or through the airline before travel. US citizens and permanent residents face additional OFAC restrictions limiting travel to 12 authorized categories (such as "Support for the Cuban People") and cannot travel purely as a tourist — proper documentation of a qualifying purpose is required. Indian nationals and most others need the standard Tourist Card plus travel/health insurance valid in Cuba.
averageFlightTime: ~15.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Havana?
faqs.answer: Most visitors need a Cuban Tourist Card (visa) arranged in advance or through the airline before travel. US citizens and permanent residents face additional OFAC restrictions limiting travel to 12 authorized categories (such as "Support for the Cuban People") and cannot travel purely as a tourist — proper documentation of a qualifying purpose is required. Indian nationals and most others need the standard Tourist Card plus travel/health insurance valid in Cuba.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Havana?
faqs.answer: November to April is the dry season with pleasant temperatures and lower humidity, avoiding the June-November hurricane risk window. Tropical climate with warm winters (highs around 26°C) and hot, humid summers (highs around 32-33°C); hurricane season runs June to November, peaking August-October.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Havana safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Havana is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Havana?
faqs.answer: The local currency is CUP. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: James Botha
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Our trip to Havana was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Museo de la Revolución was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Marco Brown
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Classic convertible car tour (Malecón/city loop) and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Mohammed Ibrahim
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Hemingway trail tour (Finca Vigía and Cojímar) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Museo de la Revolución this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Sarah Santos
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Havana is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Callejón de Hamel Afro-Cuban art tour. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Fatima Tanaka
